# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Google Compute Engine instances. Use default service account and default disk encryption settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting instances running - we'll add custom encryption keys and security hardening in phase 2.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

resources:
- name: project
  type: googleapis.com/Project
  properties:
    projectId: my-project-id

- name: network
  type: googleapis.com/ComputeNetwork
  properties:
    project: ${DATA_PROJECT}
    name: default

- name: subnetwork
  type: googleapis.com/ComputeSubnetwork
  properties:
    project: ${DATA_PROJECT}
    region: us-central1
    network: ${network.name}
    name: default

- name: instanceTemplate
  type: googleapis.com/ComputeInstanceTemplate
  properties:
    project: ${DATA_PROJECT}
    metadata:
      items:
        - keyName: start-script
          value: |
            # Your custom startup script goes here.
    machineType: zones/${REGION}/machineTypes/n1-standard-1
    disks:
      - boot: true
        autoDetach: DISK
        initializeParams:
          sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-9
      - name: data-disk
        type: pd-standard
        boot: false
        sizeGb: 10
        diskEncryptionKey: FIPS_KEY
    serviceAccounts:
      - 546889259477-compute-readonly@projects.service.account.com

- name: instanceGroup
  type: googleapis.com/ComputeInstanceGroup
  properties:
    project: ${DATA_PROJECT}
    zone: us-central1-a
    instanceTemplate: ${instanceTemplate.selfLink}
    namedPorts:
      - name: http
        port: 80
    metadata:
      items:
        - keyName: autorepair
          value: "true"

- name: instanceGroupManager
  type: googleapis.com/ComputeInstanceGroupManager
  properties:
    project: ${DATA_PROJECT}
    zone: us-central1-a
    instanceGroup: ${instanceGroup.selfLink}
    namedPorts:
      - name: http
        protocol: TCP
    targetHppPool: ${targetHppPool.selfLink}
